quotes.verbatim_quote:
Whole Tone Scales (Augmented)
There are two Whole Tone Scales: WHWHWHWH
C7, E7, F7, G7, A7, B7
quotes.why_load_bearing: Defines whole tone scale and applicable dominant 7ths.

quotes.topic: Diminished Scale construction and chord fit
quotes.verbatim_quote:
Diminished Scales
There are only three Diminished Scales
The Diminished scale fits these chord symbols: G7b5, A7b5, B7b5
also D7b5, F7b5, G7b5

Half & Whole Step Construction: WHWHWHWH
quotes.why_load_bearing: Rule: only three diminished scales mapped to altered-dominant chord symbols.

quotes.topic: Major, Dominant, Minor scales to the 9th
quotes.verbatim_quote: The scales are written in all 12 keys from the root (first note of any scale) to the 9th of that scale. The blackened-in notes are chord tones: root, 3rd, 5th, 7th and 9th
quotes.why_load_bearing: Notational convention identifying chord tones within scales.

quotes.verbatim_quote: Below are several exercises every musician should memorize and be able to play in all MAJOR, MINOR (Dorian minor), and DOMINANT 7th keys. These are basic exercises which will help you gain speed and dexterity. Begin by practicing slowly, then gradually increase speed. Strive for smoothness and slur each exercise. Keyboard and string players should play legato. After you get the feel of several, consult the ARTICULATIONS chapter. These exercises are great for warming up each day. You can apply these to ANY scale or chord, regardless of quality.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Core practice prescription governing ten basic exercises.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
1. Chromatic Scale One Octave
2. Major First 5 notes
3. Minor First 5 notes
4. Dominant 7th
5. Minor (Dorian)
6. Chromatic Scale from your lowest note to the highest
7. C7
8. C7 Dominant 7th
9. C7 Full Scale to the 9th
10. C7 Chord (root, 3rd, 5th, 7th)
quotes.why_load_bearing: Ten canonical warm-up exercises across all keys.

quotes.topic: Root sequence practice in all keys
quotes.verbatim_quote:
I strongly urge you to practice the above exercises, using as a guide any of the following root sequences to assure yourself of practice in all keys.
1. C Db D Eb E F# G Ab A Bb B C
2. C F G Ab Bb C Db D Eb E F# G Ab A Bb B C
3. C D E F# G Ab Bb C Db D Eb E F# G Ab A Bb B C
4. C Eb Gb A/Db E G Bb/D F Ab B
5. C Bb A/Db Gb F E Bb/D Db C
6. C Eb Db E D F Eb Gb A G F Ab G Bb A B C
quotes.why_load_bearing: Six distinct key-cycle sequences for full-key practice coverage.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Your voice has been with you much longer than your instrumen!
Use it to sing with then transfer the music to your instrument.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Ear-first internalization via singing precedes instrumental execution.
